title: 10 best herbal teas for dark circles
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dark circles are infusions made from various plants and herbs that are believed to help reduce the appearance of dark circles under the eyes. These teas typically contain natural antioxidants, anti-inflammatory, and circulation-boosting properties that can help improve blood flow, reduce puffiness, and brighten the skin around the eyes. Some examples of herbal teas that are commonly used for dark circles include chamomile, green tea, and rooibos. By drinking these teas regularly, individuals may notice an improvement in the appearance of their dark circles, leading to a more refreshed and rejuvenated look. This can have a positive impact on their self-confidence and overall well-being, allowing them to feel more comfortable and confident in their appearance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with dark circles because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. The caffeine in white tea can also help to constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, white tea is high in tannins and flavonoids, which can help to strengthen and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Regular consumption of white tea or application of white tea bags on the eyes can help to brighten and rejuvenate the eye area, leaving you looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: White tea is rich in antioxidants that protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, helping to maintain a more even skin tone and reduce dark circles. Catechins, a type of antioxidant, are responsible for this property.

Improves microcirculation: White tea can help improve blood flow and reduce the pooling of blood under the eyes, which can make dark circles more pronounced. This is due to its rutin content, a bioflavonoid known to strengthen capillary walls and improve circulation.

Anti-aging: White tea has anti-aging properties that can help maintain skin elasticity and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles around the eyes, which can contribute to a more youthful and refreshed appearance. The high levels of polyphenols in white tea are responsible for this effect.

Soothing: White tea has a soothing effect on the skin, which can help reduce redness and irritation that can exacerbate the appearance of dark circles. This is due to the presence of various antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, including catechins, flavonoids, and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, white tea leaves can help reduce inflammation and strengthen blood vessels, which may help alleviate dark circles.
Buds: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, white tea buds can help improve blood circulation and reduce puffiness around the eyes.
Flowers: Infused in tea, white tea flowers can provide soothing and calming effects, which may help diminish the appearance of dark circles caused by stress or fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water and let it cool down to 160-185°F (71-85°C), an ideal temperature for white tea.
Steep one teaspoon of dried herbal white tea (with ingredients like chamomile, lavender, and calendula) in the hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak two cotton pads in the cooled tea and place them on your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the cotton pads and gently rinse your eyes with cool water, then pat dry. Repeat this process 1-2 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with dark circles because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and puffiness in the eye area. The tea's natural antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid, help to improve blood circulation and reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the skin, which can help to soothe and relax the delicate eye area. Drinking lemon balm tea or using it as a compress can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy, refreshed-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm tea's anti-inflammatory effect helps reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles. This property is attributed to the rosmarinic acid and eugenol present in lemon balm.

Antioxidant property: The high antioxidant content of lemon balm tea, including rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, helps protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can lead to dark circles.

Calming property: Lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the skin, which can help alleviate irritation and redness, reducing the appearance of dark circles. This soothing effect is due to the presence of citronellal and geraniol in lemon balm.

Vasoconstrictor property: Lemon balm tea contains tannins, which act as vasoconstrictors, helping to reduce the appearance of dark circles by constricting blood vessels and minimizing the pooling of blood under the skin.

Diuretic property: Lemon balm tea's diuretic effect helps reduce fluid retention, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles. This property is attributed to the citral and citronellal in lemon balm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing dark circles and puffiness around the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant contain flavonoids and essential oils that help improve blood circulation, reducing the appearance of dark circles.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful in reducing dark circles and brightening the skin.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried lemon balm leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Strain the tea to remove the lemon balm leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with dark circles because it contains flavonoids, which are powerful antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation. Dark circles under the eyes can be caused by poor circulation, which can lead to the pooling of blood and the formation of dark shadows. The anti-inflammatory properties of pot marigold tea can help reduce swelling and puffiness, while its antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, pot marigold tea has a calming effect on the skin, which can help reduce redness and irritation. Drinking pot marigold tea or using it as a compress can help improve the appearance of dark circles and promote healthy, vibrant-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids, such as lutein and zeaxanthin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tea help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can lead to dark circles. This is attributed to the high levels of vitamin C, flavonoids, and carotenoids.

Venotonic: Pot marigold tea has venotonic properties, which help strengthen blood vessels and reduce their permeability, thereby preventing the leakage of blood into the surrounding tissues and minimizing the appearance of dark circles. This effect is mainly due to the flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin.

Soothing: The soothing properties of pot marigold tea help alleviate irritation and redness, providing relief to the sensitive skin around the eyes. This is attributed to the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, such as the essential oil content and saponins.

Emollient: Pot marigold tea has emollient properties that help moisturize and soften the skin, improving its overall appearance and reducing the visibility of dark circles. This is due to the presence of mucilage, waxes, and sterols, which form a protective barrier on the skin and lock in moisture.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the appearance of dark circles.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant are a great source of vitamins and minerals, and they can help improve blood circulation, reducing dark circles.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain essential oils that can help moisturize and nourish the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried pot marigold flowers.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with dark circles because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The flavonoids present in thyme tea can strengthen the capillaries around the eyes, preventing the pooling of blood that can cause dark circles. Additionally, thyme tea has antiseptic and astringent properties that can help tighten the skin and reduce puffiness, further diminishing the appearance of dark circles. Drinking thyme tea or using it as a compress can provide a soothing and rejuvenating effect on the delicate skin around the eyes, promoting a brighter and more refreshed appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes, which can make dark circles less noticeable. This is due to the presence of compounds such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Thyme tea contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier and more even-toned skin. The high antioxidant content, including phenolic acids and flavonoids, contributes to reducing dark circles.

Vasoconstrictor: Thyme tea has vasoconstrictor properties that can help shrink dilated blood vessels under the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles. This effect is attributed to the essential oil content, particularly the compound carvacrol.

Antimicrobial: Thyme tea's antimicrobial properties can help maintain a clean and healthy skin environment, preventing infections that could worsen dark circles. The presence of thymol and other essential oils gives thyme tea its antimicrobial properties.

Collagen synthesis promotion: Thyme tea may promote collagen synthesis, which can improve skin elasticity and firmness. This can help reduce the appearance of dark circles by strengthening the skin around the eyes. The high content of antioxidants and rosmarinic acid in thyme tea supports collagen production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the appearance of dark circles.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils and flavonoids that can improve blood circulation, helping to diminish dark circles.
Stems: Thyme stems have astringent properties that can tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the visibility of dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water in a pot for 5-10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves to the boiling water.
Let the thyme leaves steep in the hot water for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the thyme leaves.
Drink the thyme tea while it's still warm, up to twice a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with dark circles because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and improve blood flow. The antioxidants in oregano tea can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of dark circles. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of oregano tea can help to reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes, which can make dark circles appear more pronounced. Drinking oregano tea or using it as a compress can help to improve the appearance of dark circles and promote healthy, refreshed-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Oregano tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes, a common cause of dark circles. This is due to the presence of active constituents like carvacrol and thymol.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in oregano tea helps protect the delicate skin under the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of dark circles. The rosmarinic and caffeic acids in oregano are responsible for its antioxidant effects.

Antimicrobial: Oregano tea's antimicrobial properties help eliminate harmful bacteria that can cause or worsen skin conditions leading to dark circles. The active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, contribute to its antimicrobial effects.

Vasoconstrictor: Oregano tea contains vasoconstrictor compounds that can help shrink dilated blood vessels under the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles. This is attributed to the presence of active constituents like rosmarinic acid.

Blood vessel strengthening: Oregano tea's ability to strengthen blood vessels can help prevent the pooling of blood under the eyes, which can lead to dark circles. This is due to the tea's high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the oregano tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for reducing the appearance of dark circles.
Flowers: The flowers of the oregano tea plant contain bioactive compounds that can improve blood circulation, helping to diminish dark circles under the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the oregano tea plant are a rich source of essential oils, which have skin-lightening properties, making them effective in reducing the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried oregano leaves, allowing it to simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the mixture steep for an additional 5 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the oregano leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the oregano tea twice a day for several weeks to help reduce dark circles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with dark circles because it contains an active compound called glabridin, which has been shown to inhibit the production of melanin, the pigment that gives skin its color. Dark circles under the eyes can be caused by an overproduction of melanin or by dilated blood vessels showing through the skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice tea can also help reduce swelling and puffiness in the eye area, further reducing the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, licorice tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health and reducing the appearance of dark circles over time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea contains glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate dark circles caused by irritation or swelling.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of licorice tea, due to its high flavonoid content, help protect the skin from oxidative stress, reducing the appearance of dark circles.
Depigmentation: Licorice tea contains glabridin, an active constituent that inhibits melanin production, which can help fade dark circles caused by hyperpigmentation.
Vasoconstrictor: Licorice tea has vasoconstrictor properties, which can help reduce blood vessel dilation and engorgement, diminishing the appearance of dark circles.
Soothing: The soothing and calming effects of licorice tea, thanks to its anti-irritant properties, can alleviate skin issues that contribute to dark circles, such as dryness, redness, and puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant contain active compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation and pigmentation, making it effective for treating dark circles.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant are rich in flavonoids and antioxidants, which can help improve blood circulation and reduce puffiness around the eyes, helping to alleviate dark circles.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice tea plant contain essential oils and antioxidants that can help soothe and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles.

Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tea plant are a rich source of fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help nourish and hydrate the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles and promoting a healthy, radiant complexion.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root, simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Turn off heat, let the mixture steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink the tea, 1-2 times a day, to help reduce dark circles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tea helps with dark circles because it contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation. This can help decrease the appearance of dark circles under the eyes, as poor circulation and inflammation can cause the skin to appear darker. Additionally, littleleaf linden tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and promote better sleep. Adequate sleep is essential for healthy-looking skin, as lack of sleep can exacerbate dark circles. Therefore, drinking littleleaf linden tea can help improve the appearance of dark circles in multiple ways.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Littleleaf linden tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: The tea contains antioxidants that help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of dark circles. The primary antioxidants are flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Vasoconstrictive: Littleleaf linden tea has vasoconstrictive properties, which help narrow blood vessels and reduce blood pooling under the eyes, minimizing the appearance of dark circles. This is attributed to the presence of tannins.

Calming: The tea has calming properties that can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which can lead to poor sleep and the development of dark circles. The calming effect is primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils.

Diuretic: Littleleaf linden tea has diuretic properties that help reduce fluid retention, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. This is due to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in tannins and antioxidants, linden leaves help reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and volatile oils, linden flowers can improve blood circulation, reducing the appearance of dark circles.
Bark: Bark extracts have anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate swelling and dark circles.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, linden seeds can moisturize and nourish the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water and add two teaspoons of dried littleleaf linden flowers.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and strain the tea, discarding the spent flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the tea twice a day for best results in reducing dark circles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with dark circles because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. The tannins in witch hazel act as an astringent, which can help to tighten the skin and reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, witch hazel has antioxidant properties that can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of dark circles. Drinking witch hazel tea can also help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the body, which can further help to reduce the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea reduces inflammation, which can help alleviate dark circles caused by puffy eyes or skin irritation. This property is due to the presence of tannins and gallic acid.
Astringent: Witch hazel tea tightens and tones the skin, which can help minimize the appearance of dark circles. The active constituents responsible for this are primarily tannins.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tea contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage, reducing the likelihood of dark circles. The primary antioxidants in witch hazel are flavonoids, tannins, and gallic acid.

Venotonic: Witch hazel tea can improve blood flow and strengthen capillaries, reducing the appearance of dark circles caused by poor circulation or leaking capillaries. This property is attributed to the presence of rutin and other flavonoids.

Soothing: Witch hazel tea has a calming effect on the skin, which can help alleviate dark circles caused by stress, fatigue, or environmental factors. This soothing effect is primarily due to the tannins and volatile oils found in witch hazel.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel tea plant contains tannins, which are astringent and can help reduce the appearance of dark circles.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and reduce puffiness around the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel tea plant contain antioxidants, which can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage.
Buds: The buds of the plant have high levels of flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce the appearance of dark circles.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the witch hazel tea plant contain gallic acid and essential oils, which have anti-bacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it an effective treatment for dark circles.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried witch hazel leaves, bringing them to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and let the mixture simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and allow the tea to steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink the tea twice a day, in the morning and evening, to help reduce the appearance of dark circles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with dark circles because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the skin around the eyes. The tea's antioxidants, such as flavonoids and tannins, can help to strengthen blood vessels and reduce the appearance of dark circles. Additionally, yarrow tea has a cooling and soothing effect on the skin, which can help to reduce puffiness and brighten the eye area. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from dark circles and promote healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of dark circles. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Astringent: Yarrow tea has astringent properties that can help tighten the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles. This is attributed to the tannins present in yarrow.

Antioxidant: Yarrow tea's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage, which can help prevent the formation of dark circles. The antioxidants in yarrow include flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Venotonic: Yarrow tea can help improve circulation, which can reduce the appearance of dark circles caused by poor blood flow. This is due to the lactones and essential oils in yarrow.

Depigmenting: Yarrow tea may help lighten the skin, reducing the appearance of dark circles. This is attributed to the presence of coumarins and flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit melanin production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing dark circles and puffiness.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids that help improve blood circulation, reducing the appearance of dark circles.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids and vitamins, promoting skin health and reducing the visibility of dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a minute or two, so it's not boiling hot when you add the herbs.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried yarrow flowers or 2 teaspoons of fresh yarrow leaves to the hot water.
Cover and let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbs to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used herbs.
Drink the yarrow tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day, to help reduce the appearance of dark circles under your eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with dark circles because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. The tea is rich in tannins, which can help to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the appearance of dark circles. Comfrey tea also contains allantoin, a compound that promotes the growth of new cells and can help to fade dark circles over time. Drinking comfrey tea or using it as a compress can help to soothe and nourish the delicate skin around the eyes, promoting a brighter, more refreshed appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea contains allantoin, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling, important for dark circles caused by poor blood flow or irritation.
Astringent: Comfrey tea has tannins that tighten and constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of dark circles caused by dilated blood vessels.
Antioxidant: Comfrey tea is rich in rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that prevents skin damage and discoloration, addressing dark circles caused by oxidative stress.

Collagen production: Comfrey tea contains beneficial compounds like allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which promote collagen production, leading to thicker, healthier skin and reducing the appearance of dark circles.
Wound healing: Comfrey tea's active constituents, such as allantoin, support skin regeneration and wound healing, addressing dark circles caused by skin damage or injury.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins and antioxidants, comfrey leaves help reduce inflammation and promote circulation, which may help alleviate dark circles.
Roots: High in allantoin and rosmarinic acid, comfrey roots can reduce puffiness and discoloration, making them beneficial for dark circle treatment.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, comfrey flowers can help soothe and brighten the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried comfrey root and place it in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the comfrey root.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes for optimal extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tea and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink the tea once or twice a day, consistently, to potentially reduce dark circles over time.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dark circles with herbal teas is by using cold, brewed tea bags as a compress. Chamomile and green tea are ideal choices due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness. The caffeine in green tea also constricts blood vessels, diminishing the appearance of dark circles. Soak tea bags in water, refrigerate them for 30 minutes, then place on closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. Repeat this daily for noticeable results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dark circles that are treated with herbal teas include puffy eyes and under-eye bags. Chamomile tea, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, helps reduce swelling and irritation. Soak two tea bags in warm water, let them cool, and place them on your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. Green tea, rich in antioxidants and caffeine, can improve blood circulation and constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of dark circles. Steep two tea bags in hot water, let them cool, and apply them to your eyes for 5-10 minutes. Regular application can help alleviate these issues and promote healthier-looking eyes.
